Comment: Helicopter on a RealFlight heli model. The "px4" values are HIL_ACTUATOR_CONTROLS.controls[] indices and MUST match PWM_MAIN_FUNC* in ROMFS/px4fmu_common/init.d-posix/airframes/1203_flightaxis_heli. CA_AIRFRAME 10 orders the allocator motors first, then the swash servos: controls[0]=main rotor motor [0,1], [1]=yaw tail motor [0,1], [2..4]=swash plate servos 1-3 [-1,1].
DemixComment: HeliDemix converts the three swash servo outputs on rf0-2 back into the roll/pitch/collective triple RealFlight expects: roll=s1-s2, pitch=(s1+s2)/2-s3, col=(s1+s2+s3)/3, recentered to 0..1. That inverse is only exact for a swash geometry with servos 1 and 2 mirrored about the X axis and servo 3 on the axis, which is why the airframe overrides CA_SP0_ANG* to 300/60/180 instead of the PX4 defaults 0/140/220.
YawComment: rf3 is unipolar, not bipolar: under CA_AIRFRAME 10 the yaw tail is a MOTOR, so PWMSim normalises it to [0,1] already (PWMSim.cpp:78-80). Scaling it as bipolar would fold [0,1] into [0.5,1.0] and leave yaw permanently offset. Switch to CA_AIRFRAME 11 (tail servo) if you want a bipolar tail, and change this row to match.
RfModel: informational only - aircraft is selected inside RealFlight

Channels.comment: tail rotor / yaw <- controls[1] yaw tail motor
